repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.abstract: Chaffee family letters; book of remedies; a copy of LOVE LIES BLEEDING by Helen Smith Jordan, a book in which some of the letters are reproduced; a booklet "Civil War Letters Received by the Family of E.F. Chaffee, Attica, N.Y. 1861-1865," also by Helen Smith Jordan; and "Musings and Memories," by Henry Chaffee Abell, 1988.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524rareref@cornell.eduhttp://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Thomas Chaffee came from England in about 1633 and settled in Hingham, Massachusetts. He had two sons, Nathan and Joseph. Joseph married Ann Martin in 1669, and they had nine children. One son, James, was married twice and had 20 children. Most of them moved to western New York, many settling near Attica. The second son, Nathan, son of James' first wife, Berea Hayden, married Priscilla Wedge of Woodstock, Connecticut, and they later moved to Brookfield, Massachusetts. Their son William married Rachel Rice in 1807. In 1817 William and Rachel moved to a farm south of Attica. The couple had nine children (excluding one who died in Massachusetts). At least one child, Edward Franklin Chaffee, remained in or near Attica his entire life. He taught at and was a principal of the Attica Union School, and helped to pass legislation to make it a free school.

Edward Chaffee also wrote local history articles for the local press. He married Harriette E. Jones in 1845, and they had six (surviving) children: Mary Abigail, William Franklin, Harriette Emeline, Eliza Rosette, Katie Evangeline, and Nellie Josephine.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Chaffee family letters; book of remedies; a copy of LOVE LIES BLEEDING by Helen Smith Jordan, a book in which some of the letters are reproduced; a booklet "Civil War Letters Received by the Family of E.F. Chaffee, Attica, N.Y. 1861-1865," also by Helen Smith Jordan; and "Musings and Memories," by Henry Chaffee Abell, 1988.

emph.ref: Subseries Ia: Loose Letters in Chronological Order
Series II. Documents, Newspapers and Manuscripts

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Chaffee family papers, #3889. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

did.unittitle: Subseries Ia: Loose Letters in Chronological Order
Letter, printed item, bone rings carved by a soldier in the Civil War
Letters from Faith Jones about death
Civil War letters to Edward Chaffee
Letters to or from Nell Smith
Handwritten copies of letters from Mary's children
Cornell Correspondence with Helen Jordan about donated letters
Emma to Nell. Includes rattle from snake
Kate's letters to Joseph, with summaries
Series II: Documents, Newspapers and Manuscripts
"The Truth About Mary Abigail" publication packet
Story of an African American woman
Edward Chaffee "Recollection of a New England Trip"
"Musings and Memories" by Henry C. Abell
Central New York Genealogical Society "Tree Talks"
Negatives and papers for Love Lies Bleeding
